summary: Clear digital soundboard capture of a fiery European ’90 show with standout Valley Road, Victim of the Crime and Standing on the Moon performances.
review: The recording is a solid digital‑board source that delivers excellent clarity and headroom, though the keyboards sit a bit high in the mix at the very start. Musically the concert bursts with energy – short but tight versions of the songs, a debut‑ever Valley Road, volcanic Victim of the Crime and an electrifying Standing on the Moon – despite a weak Bertha that many listeners will skip.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers praise both the performance and the clear digital‑board recording, while one notes this show as the weakest of the German dates; the weighted average yields a solid 4‑star rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Digital soundboard (DSBD) with amazing clarity and headroom; early mix has Vince's keyboards slightly high but balances out later. No major drop‑outs reported.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Short, tight renditions keep momentum high; first set builds energy despite a weak Bertha, while the second set erupts with volcanic intensity and several standout moments.

band_member_comments.Keys: Vince is a little high in the mix for the first few tunes but mellows out as the show progresses.
